reviews.review_msg: Hilarious prof, loved his class. Gives very good feedback in office hours as well.

Jeremy is very knowledgeable, and his research is fascinating. I learned a lot from him. However, where it comes off the rails is when he colours some of his methods and lectures us based on his political leanings. I found myself tuning out when he did that, and he grades based on his beliefs, rather than the sources you state. Not my fave prof.
He is good with giving lectures. But the readings are very tedious or heavy. He should be considerate for full-time students who may be taking other courses.

Anyone giving dr. Brown an awful is just bad at school. His expectations are the absolute most clear out of any prof I have ever had, and it is evident by his desire for student success. This class is extremely informative about Chinese history. In order to succeed, you need to put the work in, but dr. Brown does not make it insanely difficult.

Peak prof, tbh. Course was challenging, but super fun. His expectations aren't through the roof, and it's pretty clear what he expects of you in the oral midterm. Allows queue cards for exams, friendly and approachable. Stressing again that HIST254 is NOT easy, come prepared. Do the readings, he has pop quizzes!!!

I really enjoyed this class! Jeremy Brown is super organized and makes the material feel approachable, even if you don't have a big background in  history. The midterm was a book-oral exam, which sounded intimidating at first but ended up being totally manageable.Would definitely recommend if you want a lighter history elective with a good prof!

I was a student of HIST 255. Honestly, I was a student of 255. I saw someone comment that Dr. Brown is an emotional professor while he is giving a lecture. I do not agree with that and I think 90% of Hist 255 students do not believe that! Also, I believe these "unbelievable" comments are from "one single person".
Dr. Brown is a great professor! I highly recommend taking this class with him! He is one of the best professors in the SFU History Department!
